items.descriptionHtml:
Scopely is looking for a Head of Product to join our GSN Casino game team in our Bangalore Hub!

At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play, every day - whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues, or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily.

GSN Casino is a category-leading free-to-play social casino experience with a large, mature player base and a highly scaled LiveOps business. This role is responsible for driving the next phase of growth through product innovation, operational excellence, and strong leadership

What You Will Do

Own the Business

  • Own the product strategy and performance of GSN Casino across its full lifecycle
  • Be accountable for revenue, engagement, retention, and LTV
  • Set targets, build forecasts, and communicate performance across the studio
  • Partner with the GM to define business priorities and long-term strategy

Drive Product Strategy

  • Define the product vision across core gameplay, meta, and LiveOps
  • Identify and lead high-impact product bets to unlock growth
  • Champion a strong experimentation culture with rapid Build-Measure-Learn loops
  • Leverage market trends and competitor insights to inform strategy

Lead the Studio (Cross-Functional)

  • Operate as a core member of the studio leadership team
  • Partner with Engineering, Production, and Creative leadership to drive outcomes
  • Align teams around a clear product vision and execution plan
  • Lead cross-functional decisions balancing player experience, tech constraints, and business impact

Own LiveOps & Execution

  • Own the LiveOps strategy and operating cadence for a scaled game
  • Monitor live performance and lead real-time interventions to maximize outcomes
  • Partner with CRM, UA, and Marketing to amplify feature and event impact

Build & Scale the Product Org

  • Lead and develop a team of high performing product managers and analysts
  • Lead and integrate Product and Analytics to drive a deeply data-informed culture
  • Raise the bar on product thinking, execution, and accountability

What We’re Looking For

  • 13+ years of product experience in scaled digital consumer products or mobile gaming
  • Proven ownership of large-scale live products with revenue responsibility
  • Deep expertise in F2P systems and LiveOps
  • Strong leadership experience managing senior product leaders and cross-functional teams
  • Highly analytical, with strong experimentation and decision-making skills

At Scopely, we create games for everyone - and want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us & embrace the adventure!

About Scopely

Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.

Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.

Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.

Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/.

Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com.

Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice.

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

items.descriptionHtml:
Cube is an AI-powered marketing startup founded by alumni of Stanford, Wharton, and IITs, and headquartered in Bengaluru. Backed by Sequoia and other leading investors, we’re building the future of marketing technology.


🌐 Website: https://cubehq.ai


We’re looking for a Head of Product to lead product vision, strategy, and execution across our suite of AI-powered marketing, SEO, GEO, and automation products. This role will work closely with engineering, design, growth, and leadership teams to define product direction, build exceptional user experiences, and drive product-led growth across global markets.


Application Process:

Fill this form (mandatory): https://forms.gle/DQcNpCsLVkzuYnmB6


Responsibilities

• Develop and execute product roadmap initiatives aligned with user and business needs

• Effectively communicate complex ideas and collaborate across cross-functional teams

• Work closely with engineering, design, and growth teams to build scalable product solutions

• Contribute to new product ideas and AI-led innovations across the platform

• Drive execution for product launches, features, and user experience improvements


Qualifications

• 3+ years of product management experience

• Bachelor’s degree preferred from IITs, NITs, BITS, or other top institutions

• Experience building B2B SaaS products preferred

• Strong problem-solving and structured thinking skills

• Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities

• Data-driven mindset and ability to work in fast-paced startup environments

• Based in Bangalore and open to working from our HSR Layout office during US hours


If you enjoy solving tough problems and building products from scratch, this role is for you.


Location: Bangalore (HSR Layout – US hours)

Compensation: As per industry standards

items.descriptionHtml:
The Company Behind the Products


Every business talks about “better customer conversations.” SaaS Labs builds products that actually make them happen.


What are we building? Through a powerful ecosystem including JustCall, CallPage, and ServiceAgent.ai, we empower thousands of businesses to move beyond talk and drive truly meaningful customer conversations.


Where are we now? Supported by a dedicated team of 300+ across India, Poland and the US, and the strategic backing of Peak XV and Base10 Partners, we are fundamentally redefining the art of customer conversation - and we're just getting started.


What's it like to work here? Total ownership. We’re a team that moves with purpose, builds with ambition, and loves seeing our work make a real impact.And, if you're already nodding along, let's start the conversation.


Our Flagship Product


With JustCall, we’re leading the innovation on how sales and service teams connect with their customers - using AI - across calls, texts, emails, WhatsApp, and whatever comes next! It’s a omni-channel communication system that helps 7000+ of our customers serve theirs better - with 24/7 AI Agents, on-point integrations, and other forward-looking features.


Your Mission


We're looking for a Head of Product to own all product areas in JustCall. This is a senior leadership role - you'll define strategy, drive execution, and scale product impact across JustCall, reporting to the GM of Product, Design and AI..


You'll be a strategic partner to Engineering, Design, Marketing, and Sales - translating product decisions into business outcomes, customer value, and long-term competitive advantage. You'll set the product vision, align teams around sharp priorities, and ensure we ship high-impact solutions.


Your Toolkit


  • 10+ years of product leadership in SaaS, AI/ML, or platforms with 5+ years managing PMs.
  • Proven track record building complex, multi-surface products across diverse stakeholders.
  • Experience shipping AI-powered products at scale; hands-on familiarity with agentic or multi-turn AI experiences.
  • Strong understanding of AI systems and how to integrate them into existing product portfolios.
  • Deep customer empathy and the ability to turn user needs into sharp product decisions.
  • Operational rigor - you deliver measurable improvements in quality, retention, and efficiency.
  • Excellent judgment - you know when to go deep, when to delegate, and when to push back.
  • A genuine commitment to growing people and raising the craft bar across the team.
  • Comfort thriving in fast-moving environments where priorities evolve and ambiguity is the norm.


Extras that give you an edge


  • You've worked directly in CCaaS, CPaaS, CRM, or sales engagement environments - you understand how reps, managers, and ops teams actually work.
  • You have a strong background in voice and telephony products, or have shipped communication-heavy workflows before.
  • You've navigated a platform transition - legacy to AI-native - and know what it takes to drive transformation.
  • You've influenced pricing, packaging, or go-to-market strategy alongside product decisions.
  • You've built in a multi-product company and know how to create coherence across a portfolio without slowing teams down.
  • You have founder instincts - you've operated with lean resources, made fast calls, and owned the outcome either way.


Benefits


  • Take time off when you need it. We trust you to manage your energy and schedule responsibly.
  • We don’t track hours, just impact. Work when you’re most productive, as long as you show up for your team.
  • Hybrid working model with 4 in-office days per week.
  • Competitive pay that reflects your skills, experience, and the value you bring.
  • Once a year, we also trade Zoom calls for real hugs and high-fives, gathering somewhere nice and sunny to connect, recharge, and celebrate.
  • Comprehensive physical and mental health coverage for you, your partner, and your family, because care shouldn’t come with fine print.


SaaS Labs takes pride in being an equal opportunity employer. We believe diverse teams build better products, which is why we're committed to making hiring or any employment decision regardless of race, colour, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic.


Curious about what we're building? Follow us on LinkedIn to be the first to know!

items.descriptionHtml:
Director of Product and Growth Strategy

About SmartQ

SmartQ is a global B2B Food-Tech company transforming workplace dining experiences across 14+ countries. Backed by Compass Group—a $60B global foodservice giant—we deliver intelligent, user-centric solutions for clients like Google, HSBC, Amazon, and Sanofi. With over 600 enterprise clients, we blend the scale of an MNC with the speed of a startup to drive real outcomes for global organizations.


The Role

We’re seeking a high-impact Director of Product and Growth Strategy to lead our most strategic vertical. This is a hybrid role at the intersection of product innovation, enterprise client growth, stakeholder influence, and partnership enablement.

You’ll act as a strategic partner to global account directors, a product evangelist to external stakeholders, and a business architect within SmartQ—bringing clarity, speed, and focus to how we deliver outcomes across complex global accounts.

This role is ideal for a bold, outcome-obsessed leader who thinks like a GM but delivers like a CPO.


Your Impact Areas


🚀 Product & Growth Strategy

  • Own the end-to-end product and growth strategy for SmartQ’s Global Enterprise portfolio.
  • Define, communicate, and drive a bold product vision that translates into measurable business results.
  • Identify high-leverage opportunities at the intersection of client needs, commercial goals, and partner capabilities.

🤝 Enterprise Stakeholder Leadership

  • Partner closely with Global Account Directors to align product outcomes with client growth objectives.
  • Represent the voice of product and engineering in senior-level client discussions—building belief through clarity, data, and delivery.
  • Embed partnership priorities into the product roadmap—ensuring long-term alignment with strategic alliances.

🌍 Scalable Execution & Client Impact

  • Translate enterprise challenges into scalable, repeatable product solutions.
  • Balance short-term momentum with long-term platform evolution; ship fast, learn faster.
  • Track and report product outcomes that directly support client KPIs and retention.

🔥 Team Leadership & Coaching

  • Lead, mentor, and grow a high-performing team of product managers focused on enterprise success.
  • Spend 30–40% of your time coaching—unlocking strategic thinking, execution speed, and customer obsession.
  • Collaborate with engineering, design, and operations to foster cross-functional alignment and delivery excellence.


You Are

  • Outcome-first – You prioritize real impact over shiny features.
  • Business-savvy – You understand enterprise growth levers and align product strategy accordingly.
  • Stakeholder fluent – You can influence C-level partners, internal leaders, and cross-functional teams with equal ease.
  • Momentum-driven – You bias toward progress, even in ambiguity. You know perfection is the enemy of traction.
  • Coach & Multiplier – You develop people, not just manage them.


You Bring

  • 10+ years of product experience with strong B2B exposure, including enterprise or global account work.
  • 3+ years managing and coaching PMs with demonstrable outcomes from your teams.
  • Proven success in leading product strategy across global, matrixed organizations.
  • Experience working closely with sales, partnerships, or client success teams.
  • Comfort navigating between strategic planning and day-to-day execution.
  • Bonus: experience with platform products, partner integrations, or cross-market scaling.


Why Join SmartQ?

  • Influence the future of food-tech in the workplace across 14+ countries.
  • Lead from the front—working directly with top-tier clients, account heads, and Compass Group leadership.
  • Solve high-impact challenges at scale, with full ownership and visibility.
  • Join a company where impact > input, outcomes > outputs, and momentum > perfection.

items.title: Head of Product
items.description: The CoinDCX Journey: Building the Future of Finance:At CoinDCX, our mission is clear - to make crypto and blockchain accessible to every Indian and enable them to participate in the future of finance.As India’s first crypto unicorn valued at $2.45B, we are reshaping the financial ecosystem by building safe, transparent, and scalable products that power adoption at scale.We believe that change starts together. It begins with bold ideas, relentless execution and people who want to build what’s next.If you’re driven by purpose and thrive in environments where your work defines the next chapter of an industry, you’ll feel right at home here.About the Role:This isn’t a typical Head of Product role. The expectation is not just to optimize flows, but to fundamentally redefine how trading works for millions of users. You’ll step in at a critical inflection point, owning the next phase of growth for a platform already operating at scale. This role is about expanding into new product frontiers, unlocking new opportunities, and building category-defining trading experiences. It’s a high-impact mandate to drive innovation and shape the future of trading at scale.What You’ll Do: Lead a Product Management team focused on conceiving, building, and launching user-focused, high-quality products that connect and grow the crypto economy.  Work closely with fellow engineering, design, and business leadership to define our product vision and strategy across your product areas, and deliver tangible business outcomes.  Identifying and defining KPIs and metrics for the team to set targets and drive overall team performance.  Hire, manage, and grow a team of Product Managers and help them to reach their potential.  Execute efficiently in a fast-paced, fluid start-up environment where change is constant and creativity is a must.  Driving a cross-functional team with engineering, design, support, and marketing to resolve end to end customer issues. You’ll Excel in This Role If You: Trading experience in the past, with a strong understanding of financial markets—especially Indian markets—and a genuine passion to build and scale products in this space  14+ years of Product Management experience with 4+ years in scaling digital B2C products with customer first focus  A deep understanding and appreciation for good design and user experience  A track record of building exceptional products that delight your users and produce clear business impact  Demonstrated ability to lead projects affecting many functions, including product, engineering, finance, compliance, and support.  Ability to direct major cross-functional initiatives with effective prioritization and influence  Experience collecting both internal and external customer feedback and requests, empathizing with their challenges and opportunities, and prioritizing roadmaps to optimize results  Experience in A/B testing and in working with data analytics/data science teams to get metric driven analysis  Worked in an agile environment or on the scrum methodology  Passion for cryptocurrency, financial markets, and the CoinDCX mission  Worked in an agile environment or on the scrum methodology  Passion for cryptocurrency, financial markets, and the CoinDCX mission Good to have : Experience working in regulated industries collaborating with legal, compliance, and customer support teams  Experience in software development or a sound understanding of technical architecture Hiring Process:Here’s what your journey with us looks like:Application Review – We assess for skills, alignment, and intent Recruiter Connect – A short conversation to understand you better Functional Round(s) – Deep dive into your approach, craft, and problem-solving Assignment / Simulation Round – A take-home task or live problem-solving exercise to understand how you think and execute in real scenarios Culture & Values Discussion – A conversation to understand our ways of working and how you thrive best Founder Conversation (Optional) – For certain roles and senior levels, you may meet our founders to explore strategic alignment and long-term fit Where We Work:We believe the best ideas emerge when people build together. Collaboration, speed and trust come alive when teams share the same space.With this belief, we operate as a work-from-office organisation. This role is based out of our Bengaluru office, where energy, alignment and innovation move in real time.Perks That Empower You:We believe great people deserve great experiences.Design Your Own Benefits: Flexible perks to match your lifestyle Unlimited Wellness Leaves: Rest and recharge as you need Mental Wellness Support: Access to therapy and wellness resources Learning Sessions: Bi-weekly learning and growth opportunities Ready to Build What’s Next?If you’re looking for a role that gives you direct access to high-stakes decisions, deep impact and a chance to build the future of finance, this is it.Join CoinDCX and help us make crypto accessible to every Indian, together.
items.descriptionHtml:
The CoinDCX Journey: Building the Future of Finance:

At CoinDCX, our mission is clear - to make crypto and blockchain accessible to every Indian and enable them to participate in the future of finance.

As India’s first crypto unicorn valued at $2.45B, we are reshaping the financial ecosystem by building safe, transparent, and scalable products that power adoption at scale.

We believe that change starts together. It begins with bold ideas, relentless execution and people who want to build what’s next.

If you’re driven by purpose and thrive in environments where your work defines the next chapter of an industry, you’ll feel right at home here.

About the Role:

This isn’t a typical Head of Product role. The expectation is not just to optimize flows, but to fundamentally redefine how trading works for millions of users. You’ll step in at a critical inflection point, owning the next phase of growth for a platform already operating at scale. This role is about expanding into new product frontiers, unlocking new opportunities, and building category-defining trading experiences. It’s a high-impact mandate to drive innovation and shape the future of trading at scale.

What You’ll Do:

  • Lead a Product Management team focused on conceiving, building, and launching user-focused, high-quality products that connect and grow the crypto economy.
  • Work closely with fellow engineering, design, and business leadership to define our product vision and strategy across your product areas, and deliver tangible business outcomes.
  • Identifying and defining KPIs and metrics for the team to set targets and drive overall team performance.
  • Hire, manage, and grow a team of Product Managers and help them to reach their potential.
  • Execute efficiently in a fast-paced, fluid start-up environment where change is constant and creativity is a must.
  • Driving a cross-functional team with engineering, design, support, and marketing to resolve end to end customer issues.

You’ll Excel in This Role If You:

  • Trading experience in the past, with a strong understanding of financial markets—especially Indian markets—and a genuine passion to build and scale products in this space
  • 14+ years of Product Management experience with 4+ years in scaling digital B2C products with customer first focus
  • A deep understanding and appreciation for good design and user experience
  • A track record of building exceptional products that delight your users and produce clear business impact
  • Demonstrated ability to lead projects affecting many functions, including product, engineering, finance, compliance, and support.
  • Ability to direct major cross-functional initiatives with effective prioritization and influence
  • Experience collecting both internal and external customer feedback and requests, empathizing with their challenges and opportunities, and prioritizing roadmaps to optimize results
  • Experience in A/B testing and in working with data analytics/data science teams to get metric driven analysis
  • Worked in an agile environment or on the scrum methodology
  • Passion for cryptocurrency, financial markets, and the CoinDCX mission
  • Worked in an agile environment or on the scrum methodology
  • Passion for cryptocurrency, financial markets, and the CoinDCX mission

Good to have :

  • Experience working in regulated industries collaborating with legal, compliance, and customer support teams
  • Experience in software development or a sound understanding of technical architecture

Hiring Process:

Here’s what your journey with us looks like:

  • Application Review – We assess for skills, alignment, and intent
  • Recruiter Connect – A short conversation to understand you better
  • Functional Round(s) – Deep dive into your approach, craft, and problem-solving
  • Assignment / Simulation Round – A take-home task or live problem-solving exercise to understand how you think and execute in real scenarios
  • Culture & Values Discussion – A conversation to understand our ways of working and how you thrive best
  • Founder Conversation (Optional) – For certain roles and senior levels, you may meet our founders to explore strategic alignment and long-term fit

Where We Work:

We believe the best ideas emerge when people build together. Collaboration, speed and trust come alive when teams share the same space.

With this belief, we operate as a work-from-office organisation. This role is based out of our Bengaluru office, where energy, alignment and innovation move in real time.

Perks That Empower You:

We believe great people deserve great experiences.

  • Design Your Own Benefits: Flexible perks to match your lifestyle
  • Unlimited Wellness Leaves: Rest and recharge as you need
  • Mental Wellness Support: Access to therapy and wellness resources
  • Learning Sessions: Bi-weekly learning and growth opportunities

Ready to Build What’s Next?

If you’re looking for a role that gives you direct access to high-stakes decisions, deep impact and a chance to build the future of finance, this is it.

Join CoinDCX and help us make crypto accessible to every Indian, together.

Job Description

Join the JPMorgan Chase Wealth Management team, dedicated to helping investors achieve their long-term financial goals. Our business includes Chase Wealth Management, J.P. Morgan Advisors, Personal Advisors, and our Self-Directed digital investing platform, managing approximately $1.3 trillion in assets with around 6,000 advisors.

As an Executive Director within J.P. Morgan Wealth Management, you build industry leading products for our clients and advisors. As a leader on the team, you leverage your skillset to transform product delivery and create new ways for teams to continuously deliver value to their customers. Mentor a dedicated team and lead key activities across the product life cycle, turning challenges into opportunities for continuous value and delivery. Your role will involve collaborating with cross-functional teams, including design, engineering, and data analytics, to deliver meaningful enhancements to your customers. You will leverage your analytical skills to identify opportunities for improvement and implement innovative solutions.

Job Responsibilities

  • Oversee the product roadmap, vision, development, execution, risk management, and business growth targets
  • Lead the entire product life cycle through planning, execution, and future development by continuously adapting, developing new products and methodologies, managing risks, and achieving business targets like cost, features, reusability, and reliability to support growth
  • Coach and mentor the product team on best practices, such as solution generation, market research, storyboarding, mind-mapping, prototyping methods, product adoption strategies, and product delivery
  • Own product performance and invest in enhancements to achieve business objectives
  • Monitor market trends, conduct competitive analysis, and identify opportunities for product differentiation
  • Define and execute the end-to-end product strategy, leveraging advanced analytics and optimization
  • Champion a culture of innovation and continuous improvement by designing, implementing, and optimizing workforce planning products and processes
  • Ensure data quality, governance, and security standards are maintained across all platforms
  • Present strategic recommendations and insights to executive leadership, influencing decision-making at the highest levels
  • Use AI-assisted approaches to accelerate requirement exploration, impact analysis, and backlog refinement.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

  • 10+ years of experience or equivalent expertise delivering products, projects, or technology applications
  • Proven ability to influence the adoption of key product life cycle activities including discovery, ideation, strategic development, requirements definition, and value management
  • Experience driving change within organizations and managing stakeholders across multiple functions
  • Proven leadership experience (10+ years) in product management roles at large, complex organizations, including leading cross-functional teams and driving large-scale transformation initiatives
  • Strong executive communication and narrative skills, with a proven ability to engage and influence senior technical and business leaders
  • Proven ability to deliver measurable business results through workforce optimization, supported by strong strategic thinking, communication, and stakeholder management skills
  • Experience leading high-performing teams and managing talent development
  • Use AI-assisted approaches to accelerate requirement exploration, impact analysis, and backlog refinement.

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills

  • Wealth Management or Online Investing background strongly preferred

About Us

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

Our Consumer & Community Banking division serves our Chase customers through a range of financial services, including personal banking, credit cards, mortgages, auto financing, investment advice, small business loans and payment processing. We’re proud to lead the U.S. in credit card sales and deposit growth and have the most-used digital solutions – all while ranking first in customer satisfaction.

We are here to help you manage your money with checking, savings and credit cards, combining the latest banking technology with comprehensive solutions to meet the financial needs of nearly half of U.S. households.

items.descriptionHtml:
About Rippling

Rippling gives businesses one place to run HR, IT, and Finance. It brings together all of the workforce systems that are normally scattered across a company, like payroll, expenses, benefits, and computers. For the first time ever, you can manage and automate every part of the employee lifecycle in a single system.

Take onboarding, for example. With Rippling, you can hire a new employee anywhere in the world and set up their payroll, corporate card, computer, benefits, and even third-party apps like Slack and Microsoft 365—all within 90 seconds.


Based in San Francisco, Rippling has raised $1.85B+ from investors including Kleiner Perkins, Founders Fund, Sequoia, Greenoaks, Bedrock, GIC, Elad Gil, and Goldman Sachs, and was named one of America’s best startup employers by Forbes.


We prioritize candidate safety. Please be aware that all official communication will only be sent from @Rippling.com addresses.


About the role

Tax is where trust is won or lost. Every calculation, filing, and payment is a promise to our customers and to the agencies they answer to. As Rippling scales into the enterprise and expands globally, the Tax Platform is mission-critical infrastructure: it must be accurate, explainable, and reliable at a level most payroll systems were never designed for.


We’re looking for a Director of Product, based in Bangalore, to lead Rippling’s Global Tax Platform team. You’ll own the end-to-end system that calculates, files, and pays taxes globally — across employees and contractors, across every country we operate in, and across federal, state, and local jurisdictions.

The scope covers:

  • Calculation engine, including the multi-year effort to build Tax Foundry — an AI-enabled tax calculation and policy engine that replaces our third-party dependency.
  • Filings and agency communications across all jurisdictions globally.
  • Tax payments and reconciliation — accurate, on-time, and fully traceable.
  • Upstream data audits and self-serve resolution so issues are caught before they block filings.
  • In-product explainability so every tax value can be traced to its source inputs and rules.
  • Enterprise readiness — multi-entity, multi-country, equity comp, and complex tax structures at scale.

The goal is simple: no surprises. Customers should never be caught off guard by their tax data, and the team should never be pulled into reactive firefights during filing season.


What you will do

  • Own the vision and execution for the Tax Platform across calculation, filing, payments, and reconciliation globally.
  • Lead a team of Senior Product Leads across calculation, filings, payments, data quality, and enterprise tax.
  • Drive measurable improvements in filing accuracy, on-time payment rates, weekly data accuracy, customer self-serve, and enterprise escalations.
  • Make the call on Tax Foundry while maintaining a credible third-party backup plan.
  • Build opinionated, configurable workflows that reduce reliance on Tax Ops and CX.
  • Partner with Engineering, Tax Ops, CX, Payroll, Compliance, Legal, and GTM to align expansion with platform architecture.
  • Serve as a Senior Product Leader in Bangalore, operating tightly with leadership in San Francisco.


What you will need

  • Serve as a Senior Product Leader in Bangalore, operating tightly with leadership in San Francisco.
  • 12+ years of Product Management experience.
  • Proven experience leading senior IC PMs across multiple teams.
  • Experience owning complex, multi-surface platforms with regulatory, tax, or financial complexity.
  • Strong systems thinker — you see primitives, not just features.
  • Track record of shipping both 0-to-1 launches and scaled products used by large customer bases.
  • Comfortable operating at multiple altitudes: strategy, architecture, UX, and execution rigor.
  • Strong cross-functional leadership across engineering, tax/compliance, operations, and GTM.
  • High ownership mindset — accountable for outcomes, not output.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication in a highly cross-functional, globally distributed role.
  • Payroll, tax, accounting, or fintech experience is a strong plus.
  • Strong alignment with our leadership principles: in particular, Go and See, and Are Right, a Lot.


If you don’t necessarily meet all of the requirements listed here, we still encourage you to apply because skills can be used in lots of different ways, your life experience is equally important sometimes.

items.descriptionHtml:
JoVE is the world- leading producer and provider of video solutions with the mission to improve scientific research and education. Millions of scientists, educators and students use JoVE for their research, teaching and learning. Our institutional clients comprise over 1,000 universities, colleges, and biopharma companies, including such leaders as Harvard, MIT, Yale, and Stanford. As a rapidly growing company, with offices in the USA, UK, Australia, and India servicing clients in over 60 countries, we are seeking talented and ambitious individuals to join our company.


The Role:

As the Senior Director of Product, you will lead JoVE’s product strategy, portfolio roadmap, and execution across our Science Research and Education markets. You will be responsible for shaping the long-term product vision, identifying high-impact growth opportunities, and delivering best-in-class teaching, learning, and research experiences for our users.

This role requires a strategic product leader who can balance customer insight, market trends, business goals, and execution excellence. If you are passionate about building impactful digital products, leading high-performing teams, and translating customer needs into scalable product innovation, we would love to speak with you.


Responsibilities:

  • Define and lead the overall product strategy, vision, and roadmap for JoVE’s product portfolio.
  • Lead and mentor product managers, fostering a culture of ownership, collaboration, customer obsession, and data-driven decision-making.
  • Identify key customer needs, unmet market opportunities, and growth areas through partnerships with Consumer Insights, UX Research, Sales, Customer Success, and 1st- and 3rd-party research.
  • Own product discovery, prioritization, roadmap planning, and execution across multiple product lines or platforms.
  • Define, track, and communicate product metrics, delivery KPIs, and business performance indicators.
  • Stay current with industry trends, emerging technologies, customer behavior, and competitive dynamics to inform product strategy.
  • Drive innovation by challenging the status quo and influencing cross-functional teams toward impactful product improvements.
  • Present product strategy, roadmap progress, customer insights, and business outcomes to executive leadership and key stakeholders.
  • Ensure strong product management practices, including discovery, experimentation, prioritization, launch planning, adoption tracking, and lifecycle management.


Requirements:

  • A Bachelor’s degree
  • 10+ years of experience in software or digital product management. 6+ years of experience leading and developing product management teams.
  • Proven track record of building, launching, and scaling successful products that have driven measurable business growth.
  • Strong experience owning product strategy, portfolio roadmaps, and cross-functional execution in a fast-paced environment.
  • Results-driven with an ability to work in a fast paced environment.
  • Strong analytical skills with experience defining product metrics, KPIs, business cases, and success measures.
  • Excellent communication, storytelling, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence executive, technical, and non-technical audiences.
  • Customer-obsessed mindset with the ability to translate qualitative and quantitative insights into product direction.



Why Join JoVE?

  • A competitive compensation package.
  • You will make a direct impact in accelerating science research and education.
  • Opportunity to work with global teams and in an environment that promotes innovation and collaboration.
  • Our strong promotion from within culture draws a clear path to advance your career with us.

  • Minimum 12+ years' in leading and managing agile design driven, user-centered product and development teams
  • Minimum 8-12+ years with web/mobile-based service product management experiences, in a highly matrixed, complex organization.
  • Minimum 6+ years thinking creatively, showing passion and ability to identify and deliver new product innovation, ideally in the HCM/HR space.
  • Minimum 8 years of proven track record in people management with skills on talent acquisition, performance management, rewards planning activities and development of team; proven experience in leading, coaching and mentoring teams.
  • Minimum 10 years of proven track record in project management that includes planning, tracking, managing priorities, setting expectations, process/risk/issue management and delivering commitments with focus on quality and process adherence
  • Experience of working in an international team context in distributed projects
  • Proven track record of delivering software in agile methods and techniques as well as continuous integration
  • Proven ability to partner effectively across functions with the ability to gain shared vision on challenging decisions and priority trade-offs.
  • Experience researching relevant industry trends, usage analytics, competitive pressures and applying to product strategy & roadmap
  • Strong analytical orientation and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to create a clear, concise presentation of findings, and effectively present to any level of the organization.


Bonus points if you have these:

  • Leadership: not only at the management level, but at the individual level -- having the confidence and resilience to take risks -- be self-empowered
  • Communication: Ability to communicate complex concepts to the Executive Committee as well as deep technical discussions with the Architecture/Development community.
  • Research: an inclination to continuous research inside and outside the current focus fields


Come join our Team. You’ll love working here because you can:

  • Be yourself in a culture that values equity, inclusion, and belonging and creates a safe space for diverse perspectives and insights.
  • Unlock your Potential. Participate in leadership programs designed for you to lead using your natural strengths (Leadership Excellence, Advancing Women in Leadership).
  • Grow your career with a company committed to workplace gender equality.
  • Influence the Business. Join our employee-led groups iWIN – International Women Inclusion Network, Pride (LGBTQ), Thrive (Disability), Generations and connect with a network of people who share common interests and experiences.
  • Access multiple leave options to suit your needs. Generous Parental Leave Allowance. Personal Emergency Leave. Cultural & Inclusion Leave.
  • Focus on your health and well-being. Access to Employee Assistance Program, well-being webinars, free flu vaccinations, and time off for R U OK day.


If you’ve made it down this far, we have to ask: What are you waiting for? Apply now!

items.descriptionHtml:
About Momentum

AI is the most significant shift in how work gets done since the advent of the Internet — yet most organizations are still failing to capture its value.

At Momentum — The Work OS for AI-native Organizations, founded by Bhavin Turakhia — Co-Founder of Zeta, Radix, and Titan — we are building an integrated, AI-first suite that captures work, centralizes context, and makes AI a first-class participant in every workflow:

  • Tasket — A radically reimagined, AI-native work management platform that solves for context fragmentation and process knowledge.
  • Chimera — An enterprise-ready AI assistant, co-work, and agent platform that eliminates integration tax and enforces governance.
  • Proxy — A personal agent platform that shifts responsibility and accountability to AI, keeping you in the consulted and informed role.

We are a lean team of opinionated craftspeople who sweat the details — unique in how we work and uncompromising in what we believe:

  • Maniacal Urgency — Move with relentless speed. Find non-obvious shortcuts. Innovate not just in product, but in how fast you execute.
  • Agents as Peers — AI agents are collaborators, not tools. They take on real work alongside humans in every workflow.
  • In-Person Over Remote — We default to being co-located. Learning loops are tighter, iterations are faster, and decisions take minutes, not days.
  • Delete Aggressively — If it isn't high-impact, it doesn't belong. We cut ruthlessly — features, processes, and anything that adds drag.
  • Frontline Generals, Not Backseat Managers — Leadership means owning outcomes from the trenches. We stay close to the work, the users, and the details that matter.


About the role

As a Director, Product Management, you will report into Bhavin, and lead a team of Product Managers, Designers and Analysts and work alongside rockstar engineers driving the zero to 1 motion involving vision, strategy, and execution.

We are proponents of leveraging best in breed AI tools in every step of the Product Development lifecycle - Strategy, UX, Development and Testing. This requires a very different product development mindset comprising a lean team, of senior resources, that are hands-on and AI adept, resulting in ultra-rapid iteration cycles and fast output. You will own and oversee this unique approach to the product development lifecycle, from ideation to launch to success.


Job Location

  • Bangalore, India (100% on-site)


Responsibilities

Strategy

  • Define the Product roadmap for zero to 1 product motions

User interview & research

  • Lead continuous user discovery through interviews, contextual inquiry and surveys to understand workflows, pain points, and decision criteria.
  • Synthesize qualitative + quantitative insights into clear problem statements, personas/JTBD, journey maps, and prioritized opportunity areas.

  • Product Management

    • Lead and mentor a team of product managers, designers and program managers
    • Meticulously drive each feature to completion
    • Optimize team efficiency and output
    • Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and regulatory developments to inform product strategy and roadmap.


    Growth Hacking

    • Measure Output metrics (Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Engagement) and Input metrics (Conversion Funnels, Engagement) that matter
    • Leverage Product Analytics Platforms (Mixpanel, Amplitude etc), Data Analytics Platforms (Metabase, Superset)
    • Creative Feature Ideation to come up with Features that have maximum impact on the metrics that matter
    • Leverage frameworks for Feature Prioritization


    UX

    • Drive the creation of delightful, intuitive and engaging user interfaces in collaboration with UX designers to drive user adoption and NPS


    Go to Market

    • Represent the product vision and strategy to stakeholders, customers, and partners, ensuring clear communication and alignment.
    • Partner with sales, marketing, and business development teams to drive product adoption, customer acquisition, and revenue growth.


    Forward deployment & adoption (hands-on)

    • Drive a team of forward-deployed PMs to ensure successful adoption, embedded with teams to unblock integration, workflow changes, and rollout.
    • Develop adoption playbooks: onboarding flows, enablement content, rollout checklists, admin guides, and best-practice templates.


    Skills

    1. Excellent strategic insight on conceptualizing and prioritizing ideas
    2. In depth understanding and knowledge of Statistics, Analytics, Business Intelligence, SQL, Product Analytics platforms
    3. Expertise in conceptualizing and delivering AI native products and features and indepth understanding of MCP, ACP and Agentic AI Concepts
    4. Ability to interpret complex data sets and derive actionable insights
    5. In depth understanding and knowledge of technology platforms for web and mobile development
    6. Keen eye for design and aesthetics
    7. Excellent UX and copywriting skills
    8. Excellent written and verbal communication skills
    9. Experience in conducting user research
    10. Meticulous in program management processes


    Experience & Qualifications

    • 10+ years in Product Management, in Consumer Fintech or B2B SaaS

items.title: Director of GTM Product Management
items.description: About TekionAt Tekion, we're building the only cloud-native platform that is transforming the automotive retail industry, leapfrogging it into the future and providing unparalleled customer experience. We're creating seamlessly integrated, elegant, and intuitive solutions built with cutting edge technology and powered by Big Data, Machine Learning (ML)/ AI and Internet of Things (Connected vehicle to connected devices). We’re harnessing passion, entrepreneurial spirit, deep industry expertise and the latest technologies to create something very special. We're inventing new technology along the way to overcome barriers and solve big problems, all while having a blast doing it!Role SummaryTekion is seeking a Director, GTM Product Management to lead and scale our Go-To-Market engine from Bangalore, India. This is a hybrid leadership role that spans product marketing, product strategy, GTM engineering, AI-powered content operations, and competitive enablement — reporting to the VP of Product Innovation and Design or VP of Product Marketing. You will own the strategy, systems, and content infrastructure that connects Tekion’s product innovation to revenue outcomes, building AI-native workflows that accelerate pipeline generation, shorten sales cycles, and increase competitive win rates across our Automotive Retail Cloud (ARC), Automotive Enterprise Cloud (AEC), and Automotive Partner Cloud (APC) product lines. You will also play a critical role in shaping product strategy by translating market intelligence, competitive dynamics, and customer insights into strategic inputs that influence the product roadmap and investment priorities.Roles and ResponsibilitiesGo-To-Market Strategy & Execution — Own end-to-end GTM planning for new product launches and feature releases across ARC, AEC, and APC — from Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) definition (including target dealer groups by size, geography, OEM affiliation, and technology maturity) and channel strategy through pricing alignment, partner co-marketing, and launch execution.AI-Powered Content Pipeline Design — Architect and operate automated content systems that transform raw inputs (call transcripts, win/loss data, competitor signals, CRM data) into publish-ready sales assets including battle cards, objection handlers, one-pagers, value propositions, ROI calculators, value/ROI justification content, and pre-call briefings.Prompt Engineering & Agent Prototyping — Design, test, and optimize prompt libraries and AI agent workflows for GTM use cases such as competitive intelligence extraction, personalized outbound sequencing, dynamic battle card generation, and real-time objection handling support during sales calls.Competitive Intelligence Program — Build and maintain a continuous competitive monitoring system that tracks competitor pricing, features, messaging, customer reviews, and analyst coverage — translating insights into actionable sales tools within hours, not weeks. Ensure the competitive enablement program scales through automation rather than manual effort.Sales Enablement & Revenue Tooling — Create and manage the content arsenal that sales uses in-deal: pitch decks, demo scripts, talk tracks, persona-specific email templates, and competitive positioning guides. Partner with Product Marketing teams to align enablement materials with broader campaign themes and product narratives. Ensure assets are delivered inside the tools reps already use (CRM, email, meeting prep) rather than buried in a wiki.GTM Workflow Automation — Design and deploy multi-step automated workflows connecting Tekion’s CRM, marketing automation, enrichment tools, and analytics into a unified revenue engine — including sales trigger systems that react to buying signals (funding rounds, leadership changes, DMS contract expirations) with immediate, tailored follow-ups.Win/Loss Analysis & Market Intelligence — Run structured win/loss programs, buyer persona research, and voice-of-customer analysis. Extract patterns from deal data and call transcripts to continuously sharpen positioning, enablement content, and product feedback loops.Content-to-Revenue Attribution — Implement measurement frameworks that track which enablement assets and GTM programs actually influence win rates, deal velocity, and revenue — not just views and downloads. Use data to prioritize investment and retire underperforming content.Product Strategy & Roadmap Influence — Translate market intelligence, competitive analysis, win/loss patterns, and voice-of-customer insights into strategic recommendations that shape Tekion’s product roadmap and investment priorities. Serve as a bridge between GTM and Product/Engineering, ensuring that market reality informs build decisions.Team Leadership — Build, mentor, and scale a high-performing GTM product management team in Bangalore. Set the vision, operating cadence, and quality bar for all GTM outputs. Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Sales, Solutions, and Customer Success leadership across time zones.Qualifications and Education RequirementsAt least 10 years of experience in product management/marketing, product strategy, sales enablement, or revenue operations within B2B SaaSDemonstrated experience building and operating AI-powered content pipelines, prompt engineering workflows, or AI agent prototypes for GTM functions. Hands-on comfort with LLMs, automation platforms, and structured prompt design is required — not theoretical knowledge.Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical capabilities into compelling, persona-specific narratives for diverse audiences (dealers, OEM executives, technology partners, end consumers).Strong analytical mindset with experience in content-to-revenue attribution, win/loss analysis, and data-driven GTM optimization.Experience influencing product strategy and roadmap priorities through structured market intelligence, competitive analysis, and customer insights.Experience leading and scaling distributed teams across geographies and time zones, with a bias toward building systems and playbooks that scale beyond individual effort.Bachelor’s degree in Product, Business, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field. MBA or advanced degree is a strong plus.Preferred SkillsAutomotive or DMS software product expertiseExperience building competitive intelligence programs that measurably improved win rates in enterprise sales cycles.Strong technical fluency with GTM tooling ecosystems including CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot), marketing automation, project management tools (Jira, Confluence), and data enrichment platforms.Experience designing and deploying automated outbound and sales trigger workflows that connect multiple systems into cohesive revenue pipelines.
items.descriptionHtml:
About Tekion

At Tekion, we're building the only cloud-native platform that is transforming the automotive retail industry, leapfrogging it into the future and providing unparalleled customer experience. We're creating seamlessly integrated, elegant, and intuitive solutions built with cutting edge technology and powered by Big Data, Machine Learning (ML)/ AI and Internet of Things (Connected vehicle to connected devices). We’re harnessing passion, entrepreneurial spirit, deep industry expertise and the latest technologies to create something very special. We're inventing new technology along the way to overcome barriers and solve big problems, all while having a blast doing it!


Role Summary

Tekion is seeking a Director, GTM Product Management to lead and scale our Go-To-Market engine from Bangalore, India. This is a hybrid leadership role that spans product marketing, product strategy, GTM engineering, AI-powered content operations, and competitive enablement — reporting to the VP of Product Innovation and Design or VP of Product Marketing. You will own the strategy, systems, and content infrastructure that connects Tekion’s product innovation to revenue outcomes, building AI-native workflows that accelerate pipeline generation, shorten sales cycles, and increase competitive win rates across our Automotive Retail Cloud (ARC), Automotive Enterprise Cloud (AEC), and Automotive Partner Cloud (APC) product lines. You will also play a critical role in shaping product strategy by translating market intelligence, competitive dynamics, and customer insights into strategic inputs that influence the product roadmap and investment priorities.

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Go-To-Market Strategy & Execution — Own end-to-end GTM planning for new product launches and feature releases across ARC, AEC, and APC — from Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) definition (including target dealer groups by size, geography, OEM affiliation, and technology maturity) and channel strategy through pricing alignment, partner co-marketing, and launch execution.
  • AI-Powered Content Pipeline Design — Architect and operate automated content systems that transform raw inputs (call transcripts, win/loss data, competitor signals, CRM data) into publish-ready sales assets including battle cards, objection handlers, one-pagers, value propositions, ROI calculators, value/ROI justification content, and pre-call briefings.
  • Prompt Engineering & Agent Prototyping — Design, test, and optimize prompt libraries and AI agent workflows for GTM use cases such as competitive intelligence extraction, personalized outbound sequencing, dynamic battle card generation, and real-time objection handling support during sales calls.
  • Competitive Intelligence Program — Build and maintain a continuous competitive monitoring system that tracks competitor pricing, features, messaging, customer reviews, and analyst coverage — translating insights into actionable sales tools within hours, not weeks. Ensure the competitive enablement program scales through automation rather than manual effort.
  • Sales Enablement & Revenue Tooling — Create and manage the content arsenal that sales uses in-deal: pitch decks, demo scripts, talk tracks, persona-specific email templates, and competitive positioning guides. Partner with Product Marketing teams to align enablement materials with broader campaign themes and product narratives. Ensure assets are delivered inside the tools reps already use (CRM, email, meeting prep) rather than buried in a wiki.
  • GTM Workflow Automation — Design and deploy multi-step automated workflows connecting Tekion’s CRM, marketing automation, enrichment tools, and analytics into a unified revenue engine — including sales trigger systems that react to buying signals (funding rounds, leadership changes, DMS contract expirations) with immediate, tailored follow-ups.
  • Win/Loss Analysis & Market Intelligence — Run structured win/loss programs, buyer persona research, and voice-of-customer analysis. Extract patterns from deal data and call transcripts to continuously sharpen positioning, enablement content, and product feedback loops.
  • Content-to-Revenue Attribution — Implement measurement frameworks that track which enablement assets and GTM programs actually influence win rates, deal velocity, and revenue — not just views and downloads. Use data to prioritize investment and retire underperforming content.
  • Product Strategy & Roadmap Influence — Translate market intelligence, competitive analysis, win/loss patterns, and voice-of-customer insights into strategic recommendations that shape Tekion’s product roadmap and investment priorities. Serve as a bridge between GTM and Product/Engineering, ensuring that market reality informs build decisions.
  • Team Leadership — Build, mentor, and scale a high-performing GTM product management team in Bangalore. Set the vision, operating cadence, and quality bar for all GTM outputs. Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Sales, Solutions, and Customer Success leadership across time zones.


Qualifications and Education Requirements

  • At least 10 years of experience in product management/marketing, product strategy, sales enablement, or revenue operations within B2B SaaS
  • Demonstrated experience building and operating AI-powered content pipelines, prompt engineering workflows, or AI agent prototypes for GTM functions. Hands-on comfort with LLMs, automation platforms, and structured prompt design is required — not theoretical knowledge.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical capabilities into compelling, persona-specific narratives for diverse audiences (dealers, OEM executives, technology partners, end consumers).
  • Strong analytical mindset with experience in content-to-revenue attribution, win/loss analysis, and data-driven GTM optimization.
  • Experience influencing product strategy and roadmap priorities through structured market intelligence, competitive analysis, and customer insights.
  • Experience leading and scaling distributed teams across geographies and time zones, with a bias toward building systems and playbooks that scale beyond individual effort.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Product, Business, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field. MBA or advanced degree is a strong plus.


Preferred Skills

  • Automotive or DMS software product expertise
  • Experience building competitive intelligence programs that measurably improved win rates in enterprise sales cycles.
  • Strong technical fluency with GTM tooling ecosystems including CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot), marketing automation, project management tools (Jira, Confluence), and data enrichment platforms.
  • Experience designing and deploying automated outbound and sales trigger workflows that connect multiple systems into cohesive revenue pipelines.

items.descriptionHtml:
Candescent is a forward-thinking technology company transforming how financial institutions deliver Intelligent Banking experiences. We unite digital banking, account opening, and branch solutions that power and connect digital banking, account opening, and branch solutions—creating seamless engagement across digital, remote, and in-person channels.

Our Experience-Led, Intelligence-Driven approach combines human-centered design with data, automation, and cloud-based innovation. Built on an API-first architecture, our extensible ecosystem enables institutions to adapt quickly, integrate easily, and unlock new opportunities for growth—turning every customer interaction into a moment of clarity, confidence, and connection.

We are seeking a Product Director to define the strategy and vision for our Consumer Products. This role provides leadership while delivering cohesive, high-quality experiences that communicate value effectively.

The Product Director owns prioritization and roadmap planning ,balancing platform changes, technical constraints, and business needs. This role drives alignment across engineering, product, and data teams, manages dependencies, and ensures demo strategy remains current with market trends, customer expectations, and broader business objectives.

Qualifications 

  • 10+ years of experience in software, platform, or solution leadership roles, with fintech, banking, or complex regulated-industry experience preferred
  • Bachelor’s degree required in a relevant field (e.g., computer science, information systems, engineering, or business); advanced degree preferred or equivalent practical experience
  • Demonstrated ability to define strategy and priorities for complex, cross-functional initiatives in fast-moving technical environments
  • Strong understanding of modern cloud platforms and distributed systems, with experience aligning demo or solution environments to multi-tenant, platform-based architectures
  • Experience translating customer needs, market trends, and platform capabilities into clear roadmaps and high-impact demonstration experiences
  • Excellent communication and executive presentation skills, with confidence engaging senior stakeholders and external audiences
  • High degree of curiosity and initiative, with a track record of staying current on technology trends that influence platform capabilities and customer expectations

Statement to Third Party Agencies

To ALL recruitment agencies: Candescent only accepts resumes from agencies on the preferred supplier list. Please do not forward resumes to our applicant tracking system, Candescent employees, or any Candescent facility. Candescent is not responsible for any fees or charges associated with unsolicited resumes.

items.descriptionHtml:
About our client

We’re partnering with a profitable, Series B fintech backed by leading growth investors. The organisation offers platform-level solutions for businesses, marketplaces and lending partners to reduce friction, accelerate growth, and create measurable post-integration value. We’re looking to hire a Director of Product to lead B2B product strategy and execution at scale.


Key responsibilities

  • Define and own the product vision, roadmap, and KPIs for platform and integration products (APIs, SDKs, developer experience).
  • Lead end-to-end product development from discovery through launch and iteration for B2B offerings.
  • Architect and prioritize platform standardization efforts: schema design, versioning, reliability, observability.
  • Design and own API strategy and developer experience (documentation, SDKs, testing sandboxes, onboarding flows).
  • Drive measurable post-integration outcomes (activation, adoption, revenue uplift, retention) in partnership with partnerships, sales, and customer success.
  • Collaborate with engineering on scalable architecture and delivery trade-offs; balance technical debt and new feature velocity.
  • Hire, mentor, and grow product managers and cross-functional teams.
  • Work with compliance, security, and operations teams to ensure integrations meet regulatory and risk requirements.
  • Be the product voice with external partners and investors; represent product in customer conversations and roadshows.


Required qualifications

  • 10–12+ years product experience, with 5+ years in B2B product roles and proven people leadership.
  • Deep technical fluency: API design, integration patterns, platform architectures, data schemas, and reliability concerns.
  • Demonstrated track record building and scaling platform/API products used by external partners or enterprises.
  • Strong analytical orientation: defines and tracks metrics (activation, time-to-value, LTV, churn) and uses data to drive product decisions.
  • Excellent cross-functional leadership and communication skills; experience working with engineering, GTM, and compliance teams.
  • Experience in fintech, lending, or adjacent financial services product ecosystems strongly preferred.


Preferred Experience

  • Hands-on experience with OAuth, REST/gRPC, webhooks, event-driven architectures, SDKs, and developer portals.
  • Track record of creating measurable revenue or efficiency gains via post-integration programs.
  • Experience at a high-growth startup or a successful scale-stage product organization.


On Offer

  • Opportunity to shape product and company direction at a profitable, growth-stage fintech.
  • Competitive salary and equity package

items.descriptionHtml:
Own Every Moment at NetApp

At NetApp, your ideas power innovation. We lead in intelligent data infrastructure—delivering unified storage, integrated data services, and solutions that help organizations unlock the full potential of their data, from AI to multicloud.

Ready to innovate and contribute to our path to $10B? Here, you'll collaborate with passionate teams, tackle real-world challenges, and see your impact in how customers transform and grow. If you're ready to bring curiosity, creativity, and drive to every moment, NetApp is where your journey begins.

Job Summary

NetApp is transforming how customers manage their data infrastructure through unified, AI-driven experiences. We're seeking an exceptional Senior Director of Product Management to lead our Bangalore-based manageability product team and drive the next generation of autonomous storage operations.

In this role, you'll lead product strategy and execution for NetApp's manageability platform, including AI-powered autonomous operations capabilities, hybrid multi-cloud control plane, and SaaS platform services. This is a high-impact leadership role partnering closely with engineering, design, and go-to-market teams globally to deliver experiences that eliminate operational complexity and delight customers

Job Requirements

Product Strategy & Execution:

  • Define and drive product strategy for NetApp's manageability platform, including AI-powered autonomous operations, hybrid multi-cloud control plane, and SaaS infrastructure.
  • Lead a product management team in Bangalore, building strong talent and establishing best practices.
  • Manage the complete product lifecycle for AI/ML platform services, hybrid multi-cloud capabilities, SaaS/SRE infrastructure, and trial/demo environments.
  • Develop roadmaps that balance customer requirements, competitive dynamics, and business priorities.


Platform Development:

  • Build platform capabilities that enable AI-powered agents to work seamlessly across NetApp's portfolio.
  • Deliver a hybrid multi-cloud control plane that manages NetApp infrastructure across on-premises, AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud environments.
  • Ensure platform services (IAM, security, observability) are scalable, reliable, and easy to integrate.


Customer & Market Engagement:

  • Understand customer workflows, pain points, and requirements through research, advisory boards, and direct engagement.
  • Translate customer insights into differentiated product experiences.
  • Work with sales teams to enable customer conversations and represent NetApp's vision at industry events.


Team & Organization:

  • Build and scale the product management organization in Bangalore.
  • Establish effective collaboration across distributed, global teams.
  • Partner with engineering leadership to maintain strong product-engineering alignment.
  • Set standards for product management practices and execution.


Required Qualifications:

  • 15+ years of product management experience in enterprise software, SaaS, or infrastructure products.
  • 7+ years leading product management teams, including experience building and scaling teams.
  • Proven track record of shipping successful enterprise products that customers love.
  • Deep understanding of hybrid and multi-cloud architectures, SaaS platforms, and site reliability engineering principles.
  • Experience with AI/ML technologies and agentic/autonomous systems.
  • Strong technical acumen with ability to engage deeply with engineering teams on architecture and implementation.
  • Demonstrated ability to drive cross-functional alignment in complex, matrixed organizations.
  • Exceptional communication skills with ability to influence at all levels, including executive leadership.
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field; MBA or advanced degree preferred.


Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in storage, data management, or infrastructure management domains.
  • Background in building platform services or developer platforms.
  • Experience managing services across multiple cloud providers.
  • Experience with observability, monitoring, and AIOps technologies.
  • Track record of successful product launches in competitive markets.
  • Experience managing global, distributed product teams.
  • Understanding of enterprise sales processes and agile development methodologies.


At NetApp, we embrace a hybrid working environment designed to strengthen connection, collaboration, and culture for all employees. This means that most roles will have some level of in-office and/or in-person expectations, which will be shared during the recruitment process.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

NetApp is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination based on age, race, color, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, religion, disability or genetic information, pregnancy, protected veteran status, and any other protected classification.

Why You'll Thrive at NetApp

At NetApp, you won't wait for the perfect moment—you'll make it. The early planning, the extra thought, the bold idea that turns good into great: That's how our people operate and how we continue to push the boundaries of data infrastructure.

NetApp is the trusted partner for organizations transforming data into opportunity. As the only enterprise-grade storage service natively embedded in Google Cloud, AWS, and Microsoft Azure, we empower customers to run everything from traditional workloads to enterprise AI with unmatched performance, resilience, and security.

Our culture

We celebrate mold breakers, bold thinkers, and problem solvers. We reward initiative, impact, and ownership. We provide flexibility so you can balance professional ambition with your personal life. Here, differences are not just welcomed—they drive everything we do.

If you're ready to innovate, rise to the challenge, and own every moment - make your next move your best one. Apply now.

items.description: Chief of Staff, CPO OfficeWe are looking for a strong, independent, and innovative leader to be a part of the CPO's office at Meesho. The role will report to the CPO, and collaborate with Leaders from Product, Design, Analytics & Tech to drive strategic and functional initiatives at Meesho. This role offers in-depth exposure to product management in a high-growth tech startup and serves as a premier track to transition into a Product Management leadership role based on performance.About The TeamYou will be part of the CPO's (Chief Product Officer) Office at Meesho - a central team within the CPO org that provides strategic, organisational, and executional support to the CPO and the broader Product organisation across Product Management & Design. The team works closely with senior leaders across Product, Tech, Business, Design, and Analytics to shape priorities, streamline execution, and strengthen the operating cadence of the Product org.What You Will DoProject Leadership: Independently lead strategic "big rock" projects, including long-term product roadmaps, buy vs. build frameworks, and identifying new product investment opportunities.Functional Excellence: Revamp org structures and "ways of working" to improve speed and quality. Develop tools and processes that enhance the functioning of product org.High-Stakes Communication: Conceptualize and craft concise, compelling communications for Board Meetings, Monthly Strategy Reviews, and Company All-Hands.Cross-Functional Orchestration: Build deep relationships with senior stakeholders to ensure the CPO’s agenda is executed seamlessly across the company.What You Will NeedExperience: 3–5 years of cumulative experience in Management consulting or Strategy/Chief of Staff/Founder’s Office roles within high-growth startups.Education: Undergraduate or MBA degree.Product Curiosity: A keen interest in Product Management; prior experience working alongside tech and product teams is highly preferred.Analytical Rigor: Ability to break down complex, ambiguous problems into actionable workstreams with clear outcomes.Stakeholder Mastery: Proven ability to influence and collaborate with senior CXO-level stakeholders.Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to "tell a story" through data and slide decks.Mindset: A proactive, "owner" mindset with high comfort in navigating ambiguity and fast-paced environments.
items.descriptionHtml:
Chief of Staff, CPO Office

We are looking for a strong, independent, and innovative leader to be a part of the CPO's office at Meesho. The role will report to the CPO, and collaborate with Leaders from Product, Design, Analytics & Tech to drive strategic and functional initiatives at Meesho. This role offers in-depth exposure to product management in a high-growth tech startup and serves as a premier track to transition into a Product Management leadership role based on performance.

About The Team

You will be part of the CPO's (Chief Product Officer) Office at Meesho - a central team within the CPO org that provides strategic, organisational, and executional support to the CPO and the broader Product organisation across Product Management & Design. The team works closely with senior leaders across Product, Tech, Business, Design, and Analytics to shape priorities, streamline execution, and strengthen the operating cadence of the Product org.

What You Will Do

  • Project Leadership: Independently lead strategic "big rock" projects, including long-term product roadmaps, buy vs. build frameworks, and identifying new product investment opportunities.
  • Functional Excellence: Revamp org structures and "ways of working" to improve speed and quality. Develop tools and processes that enhance the functioning of product org.
  • High-Stakes Communication: Conceptualize and craft concise, compelling communications for Board Meetings, Monthly Strategy Reviews, and Company All-Hands.
  • Cross-Functional Orchestration: Build deep relationships with senior stakeholders to ensure the CPO’s agenda is executed seamlessly across the company.


What You Will Need

  • Experience: 3–5 years of cumulative experience in Management consulting or Strategy/Chief of Staff/Founder’s Office roles within high-growth startups.
  • Education: Undergraduate or MBA degree.
  • Product Curiosity: A keen interest in Product Management; prior experience working alongside tech and product teams is highly preferred.
  • Analytical Rigor: Ability to break down complex, ambiguous problems into actionable workstreams with clear outcomes.
  • Stakeholder Mastery: Proven ability to influence and collaborate with senior CXO-level stakeholders.
  • Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to "tell a story" through data and slide decks.
  • Mindset: A proactive, "owner" mindset with high comfort in navigating ambiguity and fast-paced environments.
